text: The Stoics held that each person carries a fragment of the divine logos — universal reason — and that the rational life consists in aligning one's own reason with this larger order. Distraction, from this perspective, is not merely a waste of time but a kind of self-betrayal: to surrender your attention to trivialities is to ignore the highest capacity you possess. Musonius Rufus, the Roman Stoic who taught before Epictetus, argued that philosophy is not a subject studied at school but a daily discipline of refusing to let the lesser parts of the self govern the greater. He called this the 'practice of reason in action.'
question: How did Musonius Rufus define philosophy, according to the passage?

options.text: A system of logic that reveals universal truths
A set of arguments proving the supremacy of virtue over pleasure
A subject studied intensively at a school of ethics
A daily discipline of refusing to let the lesser parts of the self govern the greater
